originalTitle: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour
title: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour
content: Leo XIV wants the world's attention on a continent vital to the Church's future, an aide says.

originalTitle: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
title: News from Nigeria: What Happened at the Market?
content:
Hello everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa, ready to tell you about important things happening in our big, beautiful continent. Today, we're talking about something that happened in a village in a country called Nigeria. Nigeria is a very big country in West Africa, with lots of people and many different languages. It's a place full of life!

What Happened in the Village?

Imagine a busy market day. A market is like a big outdoor shop where people go to buy food, clothes, and many other things. People are laughing, talking, and selling their goods. In a village in Nigeria, people were at their market, just like on any other day. It was a normal day, full of colours and sounds. Then, something big happened. People heard loud sounds, and some reports say an airplane was flying overhead. This made many people feel scared and worried. Did you know? Markets are very important places in Africa! They are where friends meet, families buy what they need, and communities gather.

What is the Air Force?

Sometimes, countries have special groups of people called an 'Air Force'. These are brave men and women who fly airplanes. Their job is to help keep everyone safe. They use their airplanes to look after the country and make sure bad people don't cause trouble. It's a very important job to protect everyone. In Nigeria, there is a group called the Nigerian Air Force. They fly airplanes that help protect the country from people who cause problems. Simple Explanation: An Air Force is like a superhero team in the sky, using special planes to keep their country safe from danger.

Who are the "Bad People"?

Sometimes, there are small groups of people who cause trouble. They are sometimes called 'militants'. They do things that hurt others and make people afraid. The Nigerian Air Force and other brave soldiers try to stop these bad people. They want to make sure everyone can live in peace and be happy. So, the Air Force was trying to find these bad people to stop them from causing more trouble. They want to make Nigeria a safe place for everyone, especially children like you! Did you know? Armies and air forces around the world work hard to keep their countries safe from harm and to protect their citizens.

Why is This a Worry?

When the Air Force tries to stop the bad people, they use their planes. But sometimes, even when they try to be very careful, things can go wrong. It's like when you are playing a game, and sometimes you accidentally bump into someone. The Air Force also tries its best, but mistakes can happen. Reports say that maybe, by accident, the market where all the people were was hit. This is very sad news, because innocent people might have gotten hurt. Everyone wants to make sure that people who are just going about their day, like buying food at the market, stay safe. It's very important. Simple Explanation: When things go wrong and people get hurt, even by accident, it makes everyone sad and worried. We want everyone to be safe always.

What Are Grown-Ups Doing Now?

When something like this happens, grown-ups want to find out exactly what happened. They ask many questions to understand everything. They want to know if the airplane really hit the market. And if it did, why did it happen? Was it an accident? They are looking for answers. People want to make sure that such a thing doesn't happen again. They want to protect all the good people in the villages and towns. The Nigerian Air Force has not yet told everyone what happened from their side. So, people are waiting to hear from them to understand the full story. Did you know? Asking questions helps us learn and understand things better, so we can make better choices in the future.

Helping Each Other

When difficult things happen, people often come together to help each other. They offer comfort and support to those who are sad or hurt. It's important to remember that most people in the world are good. They want to help and make things better for everyone. Even when sad things happen, people in communities help their neighbours. They share what they have and show kindness. This is a time for everyone to be kind and patient as we wait for more information. We hope that everyone who was worried or hurt will be okay soon. Simple Explanation: When something bad happens, communities show strength by helping each other, like sharing food or offering a kind word.

What We Hope For

We all hope that everyone in the village is safe and sound. We hope that the grown-ups will find all the answers very soon. We hope that the Air Force and all the people in charge will learn from this and make sure that only the bad people are stopped, and good people are always kept safe. Our job as reporters is to share the news, so everyone knows what is happening. And our biggest hope is always for peace and safety for all children and families across Africa. Thank you for listening to the news today. Stay safe, and remember to be kind to each other!

originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

originalTitle: Viktor Orban swept from power after 16 years ruling Hungary
title: Viktor Orban swept from power after 16 years ruling Hungary
content: In a record turnout at the polls, Hungarians have voted out their long-serving, far-right Prime Minister Viktor Orban.

originalTitle: Rory McIlroy wins Masters to become fourth back-to-back champion
title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters to become fourth back-to-back champion
content: The Northern Irishman becomes the first player to repeat at Augusta National since Tiger Woods back in 2001-2002.

originalTitle: Oil prices surge past $103 a barrel after US announces blockade of Iran
title: Oil prices surge past $103 a barrel after US announces blockade of Iran
content: Asian stocks fall as naval blockade threat injects new turmoil into financial markets.

originalTitle: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
title: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
content: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

originalTitle: Trump's blockade threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
title: Trump's blockade threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
content: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

originalTitle: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
content: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

originalTitle: Man City have psychological edge over Arsenal - Rooney
title: Man City have psychological edge over Arsenal - Rooney
content: Wayne Rooney says Manchester City have the psychological edge over leaders Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

originalTitle: Will Romero's tears become the enduring image of Tottenham's season?
title: Will Romero's tears become the enduring image of Tottenham's season?
content: Cristian Romero's tears at Sunderland could prove the lasting image of Tottenham's season if it ends with relegation, writes Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: Will thrilling Scottish title race be decided on goal difference?
title: Will thrilling Scottish title race be decided on goal difference?
content: Rangers' 6-3 win over Falkirk extends the Ibrox side's advantage over leaders Heart of Midlothian and Celtic should the Scottish Premiership title be decided on goal difference.
